Yes — direct interaction with #32207. Both errors come from the same upstream change and the same GO term, and the fix already in PR #32211 (which addresses #32207) resolves both errors here as well.

### Why the two issues are one fix

EC:1.14.13.43 and RHEA:10836 were obsoleted in lockstep upstream — the EC entry reads `Transferred entry: 1.1.1.443 and 1.13.11.96` and Rhea split RHEA:10836 into RHEA:86147 + RHEA:86143. GO:0050246 (`questin monooxygenase (NADPH) activity`) is the only GO term carrying either of those obsolete IDs.

PR #32211 obsoletes GO:0050246 and mints two replacements (GO:7770080 reductase, GO:7770081 dioxygenase) carrying the new EC and RHEA IDs. As part of that obsoletion the diff strips both:

```
-xref: EC:1.14.13.43 {source="skos:exactMatch"}
-xref: RHEA:10836 {source="skos:exactMatch"}
```

### Both errors silenced — including the synonym-xref one

The QC rule at `src/util/ontology-qc.dl:80`:

```
error("... has a synonym xref to an obsolete EC: ...") :- synonym_xref(term, _, ec), valid_term(term), obsolete_ec(ec).
```

is guarded by `valid_term`, which is defined (line 54) as `term(iri), !obsolete_term(iri)`. Once PR #32211 sets `is_obsolete: true` on GO:0050246, it drops out of `valid_term` and the rule no longer fires — so the two surviving `synonym ... RELATED [EC:1.14.13.43]` lines on the obsoleted term (kept intentionally, since they document what the synonyms came from) do **not** re-trigger this error. Same goes for line 76 (the plain xref error), which is already moot once the `xref: EC:1.14.13.43` line is removed.

### Recommendation

No separate PR or follow-on commit needed. #32211 already lands the fix for both #32207 and #32212. Suggest:

- Hold this issue open as a tracker, close it on merge of #32211 (or mark it a duplicate of #32207).
- After #32211 merges, the periodic `refresh-obsolete-ec` PR (#32210) should be able to land cleanly — same way the periodic Rhea refresh (#32203) will.

If you'd prefer I amend #32211 to explicitly mention #32212 in the description (so the merge auto-closes both), happy to do that.

{"baseRefName":"master","body":"Implements **Option B** from #32207. Closes #32207 (and unblocks #32203). ## Summary RHEA:10836 (`questin + NADPH + O2 = demethylsulochrin + NADP+`, EC:1.14.13.43) was obsoleted upstream and split into a two-enzyme mechanism (Qi et al. 2021, JACS, PMID:34586791): | Old (obsolete) | New replacements | |---|---| | RHEA:10836 / EC:1.14.13.43 | **RHEA:86147** / EC:**1.1.1.443** — questin hydroquinone + NADP+ = questin + NADPH + 2 H+ (reductase step)
**RHEA:86143** / EC:**1.13.11.96** — questin hydroquinone + O2 = demethylsulochrin + 2 H+ (dioxygenase / anthraquinone-ring-opening step) | GO:0050246 is the only term in `go-edit.obo` that references the obsolete RHEA:10836. Obsoleting it and minting the two replacements removes the last obsolete-Rhea-xref violation flagged by `src/util/filter-rhea-xrefs.sc`, unblocking the periodic Rhea refresh PR (#32203). ## Changes **Obsoleted:** - `GO:0050246` — renamed to `obsolete questin monooxygenase (NADPH) activity`, def prefixed with `OBSOLETE.`, def-xref switched from `[RHEA:10836]` to `[PMID:34586791]` (the paper describing the new mechanism — keeps the def-xref valid while removing the obsolete-Rhea reference), all `is_a`/xref tags stripped per the obsoletion convention, synonyms preserved, `consider:` set to both replacement terms, `comment:` documents the rationale, term tracker item added for #32207. **New:** - `GO:7770080` `questin reductase (NADPH) activity` — `is_a: GO:0016616`, xref `EC:1.1.1.443 {source=\"skos:exactMatch\"}`, xref `RHEA:86147 {source=\"skos:exactMatch\"}`, def from RHEA:86147. - `GO:7770081` `questin hydroquinone dioxygenase activity` — `is_a: GO:0016702`, xref `EC:1.13.11.96 {source=\"skos:exactMatch\"}`, xref `RHEA:86143 {source=\"skos:exactMatch\"}`, def from RHEA:86143, synonym `anthraquinone-ring-opening dioxygenase activity`. Both new terms cite PMID:34586791 in def-xref alongside the RHEA id. ## Annotation impact `runoak -i amigo: associations GO:0050246` returns 2 annotations: - `UniProtKB:Q0CCX5` (gedK), PMID:3182756, EXP - `UniProtKB:Q4WQY9` (tpcI), GO_REF:0000024, ISS Both are *Aspergillus* fungal secondary-metabolism gene products in the emodin/sulochrin biosynthesis pathway. These annotations will need to be remapped by curators after this PR is released — likely most should move to `GO:7770080` (the reductase, since the NADPH-dependent step is what the older `questin monooxygenase` label most directly tracked) or split across both replacements depending on the experimental evidence. Flagging for curator attention; not in scope for this PR. cc @pgaudet @sjm41. ## Checklist - [x] PLAN: issue analyzed, intent (option B) clear - [x] PRE-VALIDATION: ontology parsed cleanly before edits - [x] RESEARCH: prior investigation in #32207 (PMID:34586791, EC transferred entry 1.14.13.43 → 1.1.1.443 + 1.13.11.96) re-verified against current Rhea data - [x] TERM-SEARCH: confirmed GO:0050246 is the only term referencing RHEA:10836; parent candidates `GO:0016616` and `GO:0016702` verified via `obo-grep.pl` - [x] DESIGN-PATTERNS: naming/def patterns checked against prior art (`aldose reductase (NADPH) activity` GO:0004032 for the reductase; `3-hydroxyanthranilate 3,4-dioxygenase activity` GO:0000334 for the dioxygenase) - [x] EDITS: standard checkout / batch file / checkin procedure used - [x] RELATIONSHIPS: weak axiomatization (`is_a` to the EC-family-level parent), no `intersection_of` — consistent with sibling enzyme-activity terms; reasoner places both new terms cleanly - [x] SPECIALIZED-EDITS: `/term-obsoletion` (split with `consider:`, synonyms preserved, no logical axioms left, term tracker item added) and `/reaction` (RHEA + EC xrefs with `skos:exactMatch` qualifier, def from Rhea equation) skills consulted - [x] METADATA: `created_by: dragon-ai-agent` and `creation_date` on the two new terms only; existing GO:0050246 metadata not touched except for the obsoletion fields; `term_tracker_item` link to #32207 on all three terms - [x] AUTOMATED-VALIDATION: - `robot convert -i go-edit.obo -f obo` — parses - `robot verify` over all 16 SPARQL QC rules — all PASS, 0 violations - `robot reason -r ELK -i go-edit.obo` — succeeds; both new terms classify under their asserted parents - `make go-edit-filtered-xrefs.ofn` (the script from #32203) — completes successfully; no `Obsolete Rhea ID used in xref` errors - [x] REFERENCE-VALIDATION: PMID:34586791 verified — Qi et al. (2021) *J Am Chem Soc* 143(40):16461–16468, \"Bienzyme-Catalytic and Dioxygenation-Mediated Anthraquinone Ring Opening\" — the paper that established the two-step mechanism Rhea now uses - [x] CHANGES-COMMITTED on the only edited file (`src/ontology/go-edit.obo`) - Closes #32207 (and unblocks #32203). ## Summary RHEA:10836 (`questin + NADPH + O2 = demethylsulochrin + NADP+`, EC:1.14.13.43) was obsoleted upstream and split into a two-enzyme mechanism (Qi et al. 2021, JACS, PMID:34586791): | Old (obsolete) | New replacements | |---|---| | RHEA:10836 / EC:1.14.13.43 | **RHEA:86147** / EC:**1.1.1.443** — questin hydroquinone + NADP+ = questin + NADPH + 2 H+ (reductase step)
**RHEA:86143** / EC:**1.13.11.96** — questin hydroquinone + O2 = demethylsulochrin + 2 H+ (dioxygenase / anthraquinone-ring-opening step) | GO:0050246 is the only term in `go-edit.obo` that references the obsolete RHEA:10836. Obsoleting it and minting the two replacements removes the last obsolete-Rhea-xref violation flagged by `src/util/filter-rhea-xrefs.sc`, unblocking the periodic Rhea refresh PR (#32203). ## Changes **Obsoleted:** - `GO:0050246` — renamed to `obsolete questin monooxygenase (NADPH) activity`, def prefixed with `OBSOLETE.`, def-xref switched from `[RHEA:10836]` to `[PMID:34586791]` (the paper describing the new mechanism — keeps the def-xref valid while removing the obsolete-Rhea reference), all `is_a`/xref tags stripped per the obsoletion convention, synonyms preserved, `consider:` set to both replacement terms, `comment:` documents the rationale, term tracker item added for #32207. **New:** - `GO:7770080` `questin reductase (NADPH) activity` — `is_a: GO:0016616`, xref `EC:1.1.1.443 {source=\"skos:exactMatch\"}`, xref `RHEA:86147 {source=\"skos:exactMatch\"}`, def from RHEA:86147. - `GO:7770081` `questin hydroquinone dioxygenase activity` — `is_a: GO:0016702`, xref `EC:1.13.11.96 {source=\"skos:exactMatch\"}`, xref `RHEA:86143 {source=\"skos:exactMatch\"}`, def from RHEA:86143, synonym `anthraquinone-ring-opening dioxygenase activity`. Both new terms cite PMID:34586791 in def-xref alongside the RHEA id. ## Annotation impact `runoak -i amigo: associations GO:0050246` returns 2 annotations: - `UniProtKB:Q0CCX5` (gedK), PMID:3182756, EXP - `UniProtKB:Q4WQY9` (tpcI), GO_REF:0000024, ISS Both are *Aspergillus* fungal secondary-metabolism gene products in the emodin/sulochrin biosynthesis pathway. name: Refresh obsolete ECs file

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
  schedule:
  - cron: '0 4 * * 1'

jobs:
  update:

    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    container: obolibrary/odkfull:${{ vars.ODK_VERSION }}

    steps:
    - name: Checkout repo
      uses: actions/checkout@v4

    - name: Refresh obsolete ECs list
      run: cd src/ontology && rm ../resources/obsolete_ec.txt && make ../resources/obsolete_ec.txt && cd ../..
    
    - name: Create PR
      uses: peter-evans/create-pull-request@v7
      with:
        title: Refresh obsolete ECs file
        branch: refresh-obsolete-ecs
        add-paths: |
          src/resources/obsolete_ec.txt
        token: ${{ secrets.PR_ACTION_TOKEN }}

# This is a basic workflow to help you get started with Actions

name: CI

# Controls when the action will run. 
on:
  # Triggers the workflow on push or pull request events but only for the master branch
  push:
    branches: [ master ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ master ]

  # Allows you to run this workflow manually from the Actions tab
  workflow_dispatch:

# A workflow run is made up of one or more jobs that can run sequentially or in parallel
jobs:
  # This workflow contains a single job called "ontology_qc"
  ontology_qc:
    # The type of runner that the job will run on
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    container: obolibrary/odkfull:${{ vars.ODK_VERSION }}

    # Steps represent a sequence of tasks that will be executed as part of the job
    steps:
      # Checks-out your repository under $GITHUB_WORKSPACE, so your job can access it
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Run ontology QC checks
        run: cd src/ontology; make ROBOT_ENV='ROBOT_JAVA_ARGS=-Xmx14G' travis_test

cat > /tmp/comment.md << 'EOF'
## Plan

The two errors are real and the fix is straightforward: **bundle the refreshed `src/resources/obsolete_ec.txt` from #32210 into PR #32211**. The two PRs are currently mutually blocking, and merging them as one diff resolves both.

### Why the unknown-EC errors fire

`src/util/ontology-qc.dl:77,79` raises *unknown EC* whenever a term carries an `EC:` xref that isn't present in `src/resources/obsolete_ec.txt`. That file is the cached output of a SPARQL query against Expasy's `enzyme.rdf` (Makefile target `../resources/obsolete_ec.txt`, refreshed weekly by `.github/workflows/refresh_obsolete_ec_file.yml`). The currently-committed copy on master predates the EC release that introduced 1.1.1.443 and 1.13.11.96, so the QC sees them as "unknown" rather than "valid".

### Why this is the same fix the periodic refresh is trying to land

PR #32210 (`Refresh obsolete ECs file`, branch `refresh-obsolete-ecs`) is the scheduled weekly PR. Its diff against master:

- adds `EC:1.1.1.443	false`
- adds `EC:1.13.11.96	false`
- flips `EC:1.14.13.43` from `false` → `true`
- (plus ~10 other unrelated new ECs across the file)

And PR #32210 itself is failing QC for the **same** reason that originally opened this issue:

```
ERROR: <http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0050246> has an xref to an obsolete EC: "EC:1.14.13.43"
ERROR: <http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0050246> has a synonym xref to an obsolete EC: "EC:1.14.13.43"
```

So we have a chicken-and-egg: #32210 needs #32211 to obsolete GO:0050246 first; #32211 needs #32210 to teach the QC about EC:1.1.1.443 and EC:1.13.11.96. Neither can land alone.

### Why bundling is safe

Once both diffs are on the same branch:

- `GO:0050246` becomes `is_obsolete: true`, so `valid_term` no longer holds for it and the `obsolete EC` rules (`ontology-qc.dl:76,78,80`) don't fire on the legacy `EC:1.14.13.43` references — exactly the mechanism described in my earlier comment.
- The two new EC ids are present in `obsolete_ec.txt` with `false`, so the `unknown EC` rules (`ontology-qc.dl:77,79`) don't fire on `GO:7770080` / `GO:7770081`.
- The unrelated ~10 other new EC rows in #32210's diff are pure additions to a reference file and don't affect any other QC rule — same content the scheduled job would land regardless.

### Proposed action

1. On the `dragon-ai-agent-issue-32207-run8885` branch (PR #32211), copy `src/resources/obsolete_ec.txt` from `refresh-obsolete-ecs` (PR #32210) and commit it. Single extra commit, two files touched in the PR (`go-edit.obo` + `obsolete_ec.txt`).
2. Re-run CI on #32211 — both `unknown EC` errors should clear, and the `obsolete EC` errors on GO:0050246 stay suppressed by the obsoletion.
3. After #32211 merges, close #32210 as superseded (the next weekly run on Monday will re-open it cleanly if Expasy ships anything new in the interim).
4. Close this issue (#32212) on merge of #32211, since the same diff resolves it.

If you want me to go ahead and apply step 1 now, say the word and I'll push the commit to the existing PR branch.

content.text:
Posted the plan on issue #32212.

**Diagnosis:** The QC's `ontology-qc.dl` "unknown EC" rule fires whenever an EC xref isn't in `src/resources/obsolete_ec.txt`. That file is the cached output of a SPARQL query against Expasy and is on a weekly refresh (PR #32210 is the open weekly refresh, which adds `EC:1.1.1.443` + `EC:1.13.11.96` and flips `EC:1.14.13.43` to obsolete). PR #32210 is itself blocked by the *same* original error in #32212 — so #32210 and #32211 are mutually blocking.

**Plan:** Bundle the refreshed `obsolete_ec.txt` from #32210 into PR #32211. Once GO:0050246 is obsolete and the file knows about the two new ECs, all four error variants disappear in one diff. Asked @balhoff whether to go ahead and push that commit.
